title: Usopp’s Village Comes Into View
description: On pages 7–10, the Syrup Village story develops through Usopp, Kaya, Kuro, Jango, and the Straw Hats as the danger behind the village’s peaceful surface becomes clearer.
scene: This scene follows the tension around Usopp’s village: lies, loyalty, hidden plans, and the growing clash with the Black Cat Pirates.

dialogue.speaker: Jango
dialogue.text: I know interfering with you making friends...

dialogue.speaker: Jango
dialogue.text: ...is none of this lowly servant's business...

dialogue.speaker: Jango
dialogue.text: He has a bad reputa- lion in the village...

dialogue.speaker: Jango
dialogue.text: If... If anything happened to you...

dialogue.speaker: Jango
dialogue.text: I would feel like I had betrayed your parents!

dialogue.speaker: Jango
dialogue.text: I know that what I said before was a little extreme...

dialogue.speaker: Jango
dialogue.text: Do you hate me because of it?

dialogue.speaker: Kaya
dialogue.text: I should thank you for taking care of me,

dialogue.speaker: Kaya
dialogue.text: USOPPP-SAN is NOT a BAD PERSON AT ALL!

dialogue.speaker: Kaya
dialogue.text: You... Why wont you listen to my expla- Nation!?

dialogue.speaker: Kaya
dialogue.text: So what if I don't listen!?

dialogue.speaker: Kaya
dialogue.text: Everytime something happens, he always goes there!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Yeah! And makes tons of noise when he shows up!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: There's a man that's...... back- wards!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: There's a weird guy, he walks backwards!!!

summary: The part advances the Syrup Village arc by tying Usopp’s reputation and Kaya’s safety to the threat posed by Kuro and his crew.
